problem: An equilateral triangle has an area of $64\sqrt{3}$ $\text{cm}^2$. If each side of the triangle is decreased by 4 cm, by how many square centimeters is the area decreased?

classified_questions.question: Can you describe the properties of an equilateral triangle?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How can we find the area of an equilateral triangle?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: If the side length of an equilateral triangle is decreased by $4$ cm, what is the new side length of the triangle?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How can we use the new side length to find the new area of the triangle?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How does the decrease in side length affect the area of the triangle?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you generalize this concept, and discuss how the area of any polygon changes when its side lengths are altered?
classified_questions.label: e
